metadata.description: Computes k-WL (k=1,2,3) certificates for molecular graphs, measures collision rates and conditional variance (Bayes error floor), and assigns each (dataset, property) pair to a 2×2 typology quadrant.

examples.input: Dataset: BBBP | Property: p_np | N molecules: 2039 | Total variance: 0.1797 | Std: 0.4239
examples.output:
Typology: 3D-geometry-limited
k=1: collision_rate=0.1429, variance_floor=0.0202
k=2: collision_rate=0.1489, variance_floor=0.0182
k=3: collision_rate=0.1505, variance_floor=0.0182
Reasoning: High k=1 collision rate + high k=3 variance floor. Collisions persist even at k=3; irreducible variance from 3D geometry dominates — graph topology alone is insufficient.

examples.input: Dataset: ESOL | Property: measured log solubility in mols per litre | N molecules: 1128 | Total variance: 4.3912 | Std: 2.0955
examples.output:
Typology: 3D-geometry-limited
k=1: collision_rate=0.0916, variance_floor=0.0043
k=2: collision_rate=0.0000, variance_floor=0.0002
k=3: collision_rate=0.0000, variance_floor=0.0001
Reasoning: High k=1 collision rate + high k=3 variance floor. Collisions persist even at k=3; irreducible variance from 3D geometry dominates — graph topology alone is insufficient.
examples.metadata_property: measured log solubility in mols per litre

examples.input: Dataset: FreeSolv | Property: expt | N molecules: 642 | Total variance: 14.7827 | Std: 3.8448
examples.output:
Typology: WL-bottlenecked
k=1: collision_rate=0.0690, variance_floor=0.0057
k=2: collision_rate=0.0000, variance_floor=0.0000
k=3: collision_rate=0.0000, variance_floor=0.0000
Reasoning: High k=1 collision rate + low k=3 variance floor. WL expressiveness is the bottleneck — upgrading from 1-WL to higher-k GNNs should yield >10% relative error reduction.

examples.input: Dataset: HIV | Property: HIV_active | N molecules: 4998 | Total variance: 0.0356 | Std: 0.1888
examples.output:

k=1: collision_rate=0.0749, variance_floor=0.0410
k=2: collision_rate=0.0000, variance_floor=0.0000
k=3: collision_rate=0.0000, variance_floor=0.0000
Reasoning: High k=1 collision rate + low k=3 variance floor. WL expressiveness is the bottleneck — upgrading from 1-WL to higher-k GNNs should yield >10% relative error reduction.

examples.input: Dataset: Lipophilicity | Property: exp | N molecules: 4200 | Total variance: 1.4469 | Std: 1.2029
examples.output:
Typology: 3D-geometry-limited
k=1: collision_rate=0.1092, variance_floor=0.0037
k=2: collision_rate=0.0244, variance_floor=0.0006
k=3: collision_rate=0.0171, variance_floor=0.0005
Reasoning: High k=1 collision rate + high k=3 variance floor. Collisions persist even at k=3; irreducible variance from 3D geometry dominates — graph topology alone is insufficient.

examples.input: Dataset: QM9 | Property: A | N molecules: 50000 | Total variance: 8766963.8712 | Std: 2960.9059
examples.output:
Typology: WL-sufficient
k=1: collision_rate=0.0000, variance_floor=0.0000
k=2: collision_rate=0.0000, variance_floor=0.0000
k=3: collision_rate=0.0000, variance_floor=0.0000
Reasoning: Low k=1 collision rate + low k=3 variance floor. 1-WL GINs are already near-optimal; minimal improvement expected from higher-k GNNs.

examples.input: Dataset: QM9 | Property: B | N molecules: 50000 | Total variance: 6.4398 | Std: 2.5377
examples.output:
Typology: noise-dominated
k=1: collision_rate=0.0034, variance_floor=0.0063
k=2: collision_rate=0.0146, variance_floor=0.0006
k=3: collision_rate=0.0334, variance_floor=0.0001
Reasoning: Low k=1 collision rate + high k=3 variance floor. Low WL-collision signal but high residual variance — measurement noise or stochastic processes dominate prediction error.

examples.input: Typology quadrant: 3D-geometry-limited | Count: 10 | Description: High k=1 collision rate + high k=3 variance floor. Collisions persist even at k=3; irreducible variance from 3D geometry dominates — graph topology alone is insufficient.
examples.output: Properties in this quadrant (10 total): BBBP/p_np, ESOL/measured log solubility in mols per litre, Lipophilicity/exp, QM9/C, QM9/cv...

examples.input: Typology quadrant: WL-bottlenecked | Count: 2 | Description: High k=1 collision rate + low k=3 variance floor. WL expressiveness is the bottleneck — upgrading from 1-WL to higher-k GNNs should yield >10% relative error reduction.
